concepts.title: Functions of Central Bank
concepts.description: Central bank performs four main functions: monetary management, regulating money supply, maintaining foreign exchange and acting as a banker to the government.

concepts.title: Money Multiplier
concepts.description: Money multiplier is the ratio of money supply (M3) to currency in circulation (C). It is calculated by dividing M3 by C.

concepts.title: Types of Commercial Banks
concepts.description: There are two types of commercial banks: Public Sector Banks (PSBs) and Private Sector Banks (PSBs). PSBs are owned and controlled by the government, while PSBs are privately owned and operated for profit.

concepts.title: Functions of Commercial Banks
concepts.description: You can remember the functions of commercial banks using the following mnemonic: 'ACCEPT' - Accepting deposits, Creating credit, Collecting cheques, Exchanging currency, Providing loans, Transferring funds.

concepts.title: Difference between Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and State Bank of India (SBI)
concepts.description: Students often get confused between RBI and SBI. RBI is the central bank of India, while SBI is a public sector bank. RBI regulates and supervises all banks in the country, whereas SBI provides banking services to its customers.

concepts.title: Monetary Policy Instruments
concepts.description: Monetary policy instruments include Bank Rate, Open Market Operations (OMO), Reserve Requirements, and Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR).

concepts.title: Role of Commercial Banks in Money Creation
concepts.description: Commercial banks play a crucial role in money creation by accepting deposits, creating credit, and providing loans to customers. This process increases the money supply in the economy.

concepts.title: Functions of Commercial Banks
concepts.description: Commercial banks perform several functions, including accepting deposits, creating credit, collecting cheques, exchanging currency, providing loans, and transferring funds.
